mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0% within a single month, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined index of INDPRO and HOUST declines yields positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of observations, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, rising real money supply fails to support equities due to anticipated policy tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising RSAFS, or the conditional IC drops below 0.02 when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces recession risk premiums and expands corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth quarters consistently produce negative 60-day forward returns, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC never exceeds 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 while the unemployment rate remains above 9.0%, it signals a structural labor market mismatch that suppresses wage-driven consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in > 50% of months meeting the payroll/unemployment threshold, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy where the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25%, a decline in industrial production directly translates to lower corporate revenues, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high FEDFUNDS periods yield positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the conditional correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns flips sign.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm employment reflects broad-based economic resilience, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth months consistently produce negative 20-day forward returns, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.
